title: More Developers =/= Faster? Why Teams DON'T Scale! #shorts
summary: Simply adding more developers to engineering teams often slows progress due to communication overhead and coordination complexity. Effective scaling requires smart organizational design, not just increasing headcount.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof argues that adding developers to an engineering team often slows progress rather than speeding it, because communication overhead and coordination complexity grow as headcount rises. The point matters for anyone making staffing decisions under the assumption that more people directly translate to faster delivery. The takeaway: effective scaling depends on smart organizational design rather than raw headcount, since larger teams hit diminishing returns.
